aiFindings.title: Insecure Random Number Generation
aiFindings.description: Using Math.random() for security-sensitive operations is predictable and insecure.
aiFindings.fix: Use crypto.randomBytes() or a cryptographically secure random number generator.

vulnerabilities.title: Security issue detected
vulnerabilities.fix: This likely means the code is not valid JavaScript/TypeScript or uses syntax not supported by the scanner. Detected PHP code. Use a PHP security scanner like phpstan/phpcs-security or roave/security-advisories.

input.snippet:
import pickle
import base64

data = input("Enter serialized object: ")

decoded = base64.b64decode(data)
obj = pickle.loads(decoded)

print(obj)

input.snippet:
<?php
if(isset($_GET['ip'])) {
    $ip = $_GET['ip'];
    system("ping -c 3 " . $ip);
}
?>

from flask import Flask, request
import sqlite3

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route('/login')
def login():
    username = request.args.get("username")
    password = request.args.get("password")

    conn = sqlite3.connect("users.db")
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    query = f"SELECT * FROM users WHERE username='{username}' AND password='{password}'"
    cursor.execute(query)

    result = cursor.fetchone()

    if result:
        return "Login successful"
    else:
        return "Login failed"

vulnerabilities.title: security/detect-child-process
vulnerabilities.description: Found child_process.exec() with non Literal first argument

aiFindings.title: Unsafe use of eval()
aiFindings.description: eval() can execute arbitrary strings as code, which is dangerous when used with untrusted input.
aiFindings.fix: Avoid eval(); use safer alternatives or parse expressions safely.

aiFindings.title: Command execution via exec()
aiFindings.description: Using exec() can run arbitrary shell commands; do not execute user-provided input.
aiFindings.fix: Avoid exec(); use safer alternatives or validate/whitelist input before executing.

vulnerabilities.title: Unsafe use of innerHTML
vulnerabilities.description: Assigning to innerHTML can lead to cross-site scripting (XSS) if user-controlled content is injected.
vulnerabilities.fix: Use safer DOM APIs like textContent or a templating library that auto-escapes user input.

vulnerabilities.title: Unsafe use of document.write
vulnerabilities.description: document.write can be abused to inject malicious scripts and is generally unsafe in modern web apps.
vulnerabilities.fix: Avoid document.write; use DOM manipulation or templating instead.

vulnerabilities.title: Command execution via exec()
vulnerabilities.description: Using exec() can run arbitrary shell commands; do not execute user-provided input.
vulnerabilities.fix: Avoid exec(); use safer alternatives or validate/whitelist input before executing.
